foundation.title: STRING
foundation.subtitle: Modern Text Objects
foundation.content: C++ uses the std::string class. Unlike C's char arrays, std::string is a dynamic object that manages its own memory and size.
foundation.scenario: A Smart Locker. You don't have to choose a size at the start. If you add more books, the locker automatically expands to fit them.

examples.title: Creation
examples.explanation: Defining a modern string object using the std::string type.

examples.title: Chaining
examples.explanation: Adding text to the end of a string easily using the + or += symbol.

examples.title: Access Char
examples.explanation: Retrieving a character at index 0 precisely using its [] position.

foundation.title: FUNCTIONS
foundation.subtitle: Code Capsules
foundation.content: Functions in C++ are reusable capsules of logic. They define what goes in (Parameters) and what results come out (Return).
foundation.scenario: A Coffee Machine. You press a button (Call), it takes water and beans (Inputs), and produces a cup (Output).

examples.title: The Prototype
examples.explanation: Telling C++ that a function exists at the top of the file.

examples.title: The Definition
examples.explanation: Writing the actual logic inside the function's body.

examples.title: The Call
examples.explanation: Running the code capsules by its name with parentheses.

foundation.title: PARAMETERS
foundation.subtitle: Data Slots
foundation.content: Parameters allow you to pass data into your functions. C++ supports passing by Value (copy) or by Reference (original).
foundation.scenario: An Electric Oven. The 'temp' is a parameter. You set it to 200 (Argument) to bake, or 50 to warm. You pass it a number.

examples.title: Single Input
examples.explanation: Passing one piece of data to become a local variable inside the function.

examples.title: Multi-Input
examples.explanation: Separating multiple data slots with commas for complex tasks.

examples.title: Result Return
examples.explanation: Calculating a value and sending it back from the function slot.
examples.code: int add(int a, int b) { return a+b; }

gameData.hint: Needs a return type like void.
